target.name: Askari Defense
target.founder: Robbie van Zyl, Marc van Zyl, Benjamin Airdo

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Askari Defense is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector that has raised $1.54M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 total peers—2 public and 8 private—spanning the same sector and adjacent development stages. The private comp set includes Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety; public comparables are tracked via DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $1.54M raised as a proxy base, normalized to $2M, and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $4M. The peer-median method references 4 direct comparables spanning Seed and Series A stages: Tenna Systems at $60M, Ares Industries at $150M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Stark Defence at $500M. This produces a median comparable valuation of $225M, with a range of $60M–$500M. These approaches reflect the significant valuation dispersion common in early-stage defense tech, where comparable companies span an order of magnitude.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $3M to $259M, with a median implied value of $114M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Askari's competitive positioning within its peer set and the stage-specific comparability challenges when one method anchors to capital raised while the other anchors to later-stage funded peers. Key uncertainties center on technology differentiation relative to named competitors and the timing and terms of any near-term financing.
